MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-15-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jamelle Brown (510.384.4434) appears In Propria Persona as self-represented plaintiff/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF JAMELLE BROWN MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adjudication is DENIED and her alternative motion for summary adjudication is DENIED as to all causes of action. Ms. Brown's failure to provide any evidence of damages on any of her claims precludes granting summary judgment in her favor or summary adjudication in her favor on any of her claims seeking damages. Moreover, Ms. Brown is not entitled to summary adjudication of her second cause of action a civil harassment restraining order against defendant Shamika Guidry per CCP 527.6. The Local Rules require that all requests for civil harassment restraining orders be made in the civil harassment court, currently department 514. CCP 527.6 sets forth an expedited procedure for obtaining such a restraining order and appears to require a hearing and nothing in that statute states or suggests that a civil harassment order can be granted on a summary motion per CCP 437c. There are a few additional reasons why Ms. Brown is not entitled to summary adjudication on her third, fifth and sixth causes of action. As to the third cause of action, Ms. Brown failed to satisfy her initial burden because she has not shown that Ms. Guidry is a "claimant" as defined by Civil Code 1798.92(a). There are no facts indicating Ms. Guidry has or purports to have a claim for money or interest in property in connection with a transaction procured through identity theft. As to the fifth cause of action, Ms. Brown failed to satisfy her initial burden because there is no evidence showing that Ms. Guidry was actively involved in bringing or continuing lawsuits that ended in Ms. Brown's favor. As to the sixth cause of action, Ms. Brown failed to meet her initial burden on her IIED claim because she has not shown that she suffered severe emotional distress. Ms. Brown's request that the cross-complaint be dismissed is denied. Ms. Brown failed to properly provide notice that she moves for summary judgment on the cross-complaint. She has also failed to comply with California Rule of Court 3.1350(d) and set forth any facts showing she is entitled to summary judgment on the claims in Ms. Guidry's cross-complaint. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org. (D302)
